comment: Phase 10c exemplars — PENDING USER GATE REVIEW. Digestibility register (the tense-usage panels are the standard): short declaratives, 1-2 sentence bodies, bullets/pairs carry detail, rule first then why. These ship verbatim AND are the few-shot exemplars in gen_conjugation.py / gen_grammar.py. The 10b exemplars (etre, attendre, gender_articles, subjonctif) were removed — those units regenerate through the model like everything else.
voice_note: Register target from device review: the 'When to use it' tense panels. What was wrong with 10b: textbook-dense paragraphs, unattributed metaphor, sentences needing a re-read.

conj_aller.body: Aller means 'to go'. It also builds the future: aller + infinitive is spoken French for 'going to'.
Vais, vas, and va cover je, tu, il, and on. Everything you say daily sounds like 'va'.

examples.french: je vais, tu vas, il va, on va

conj_aller.body: Ça va ? runs on aller. French asks how you're going, not how you are.
Aller is a movement verb, so the past uses être: je suis allé. Street says j'suis allé.

examples.french: On est allés au resto hier.
examples.english: We went to the restaurant yesterday.

gram_connectors.body: Du coup means 'so'. It chains any two thoughts, and it fills half the gaps in real speech.

examples.french: J'étais fatigué, du coup j'suis rentré.
examples.english: I was tired, so I went home.

gram_connectors.body: Both mean 'so'. Donc draws a conclusion; alors pushes the story forward, or buys time as alors…
Parce que is 'because' — everyone says it passke. For contrast you have three strengths:
bullets: par contre — on the other hand (softer): par contre, c'est loin
même si — even if (concedes): même si t'as raison

gram_connectors.body: D'abord starts, après and ensuite continue, bref lands it. Bref cuts the rest: 'long story short'.
En fait means 'actually'. It opens a correction of what was just said or assumed.

gram_connectors.header: This is what fluency sounds like
gram_connectors.body: Connectors buy thinking time. A fluent hesitation is du coup… or alors…, not silence.
